meta.title: Angular Setup Guide – Node.js, Angular CLI & Project Initialization (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to set up your development environment for Angular. Install Node.js, the Angular CLI, and create your first workspace with professional configurations.

content.value: To begin building with Angular, you need a robust local development environment. As a Technical Lead, ensuring your environment matches your production build is key to avoiding 'it works on my machine' issues. The Angular ecosystem relies heavily on **Node.js** and **NPM** (Node Package Manager) to manage dependencies and build tools.

Before installing Angular, you must have Node.js installed. Angular typically requires an **LTS (Long Term Support)** version. Installing Node.js automatically installs NPM, which Angular uses to fetch libraries like RxJS and Zone.js.

# Check your current versions
node -v
npm -v

The **Angular Command Line Interface (CLI)** is the backbone of Angular development. It handles project initialization, code generation (components, services), and the build process. You install it globally using NPM.

# Install Angular CLI globally
npm install -g @angular/cli

# Verify the installation
ng version

With the CLI installed, you can generate a new workspace. The CLI will ask you configuration questions regarding CSS preprocessors (like SCSS) and whether you want to enable Server-Side Rendering (SSR).

# Create a new project named 'revo-web'
ng new revo-web

# Navigate into the folder
cd revo-web

# Start the development server
ng serve --open
A standard Angular workspace is organized to support modularity and scalability.

rows: Contains the main logic, components, and modules.
Static files like images, icons, and configuration JSONs.
CLI configuration for build, serve, and test commands.
Lists project dependencies and custom scripts.
TypeScript compiler settings for the project.

faq.question: What is the difference between 'npm install' and 'ng new'?
faq.answer: 'npm install' fetches specific libraries into an existing project. 'ng new' is a scaffolding command that creates the entire directory structure, configuration files, and initial boilerplate for a fresh Angular app.

faq.question: Why use SCSS instead of plain CSS?
faq.answer: As an architect, SCSS is recommended for large projects because it supports variables, nesting, and mixins, making your styles more maintainable and DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself).

faq.question: How do I fix 'ng: command not found'?
faq.answer: This usually means your NPM global binaries folder isn't in your system's PATH. You may need to add the path manually or reinstall the CLI with administrative privileges.
